manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: horse, mama, job, situation, key, pain, road, attention, bar.
Your opponent's team words are: water, girl, couple, floor, evidence, club, honey, death.
Distractor words are: school, doctor, world, lord, uncle, bed, stuff.
The kill words are: window.

message.content:
CLUE: shift
TARGETS: job, road, key, attention, situation
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue relating to some of our team's words, avoiding anything that is opponent or distractor or kill. The clue must not be any of the words in the lists or contain parts of them. Must be single word. Provide CLUE: WORD and TARGETS: list.

Our words: horse, mama, job, situation, key, pain, road, attention ... bar.

We can try clue "traffic" relates to road, maybe bar (traffic bar? no), key? maybe "stress" relates to pain, job, situation, attention... But we must be careful not to hint opponent words like water, girl, couple, floor, evidence, club, honey, death. Also not distractor.

Clue "career" -> job, maybe situation? Not sure. But might be too broad.

Clue "mountain" maybe road, horse? Not.

Clue "signal" relates to attention, bar? hmm.

Clue "tension" relates to pain, situation, attention? Might be ambiguous.

Clue "steering" relates to road, horse (steer), key? maybe.

Clue "saddle" relates to horse, maybe bar? Not.

Better clue "shift": job (shift work), road (shifts lanes), key (shift key), attention (shift attention), maybe situation (shift situation). That's many! But does "shift" contain part of any words? No. It's okay.

But risk: "shift" might also be associated with "girl" (shift? no) "club" (shift? no). Should be safe.

So we can list targets: job, road, key, attention, situation, maybe also "pain"? shift pain? not.

But clue must be related to at least one. It's okay to list subset.

Let's choose "shift" and include job, road, key, attention, situation. Provide:

CLUE: shift
TARGETS: job, road, key, attention, situation

Check that none of these are opponent or distractor etc. Good.

Make sure clue is single-word. Provide format.

stuff, couple, floor, honey, doctor, horse, road, girl, club, mama, bar, uncle, school, attention, job, pain, world, bed, key, window, evidence, death, lord, situation, water
